seoTitle: How to Sync Google Calendar With Slack (2026)
metaDescription: Connect Google Calendar to Slack to auto-update your status during meetings and respond to invites directly from chat messages.
intro: Keeping your schedule visible to your team without constant app-switching starts with linking Google Calendar to Slack. This guide covers installing the official app, authorizing your account, and turning on automatic status syncing.

steps.heading: Open the Slack App Directory
steps.body: Navigate to the left sidebar and launch the App Marketplace from your Slack workspace.

steps.heading: Search for Google Calendar
steps.body: Type 'Google Calendar' into the search field and select the verified app listing.

steps.heading: Add the app to your workspace
steps.body: Click 'Add to Slack' to begin the installation and deployment process.

steps.heading: Authorize your Google account
steps.body: Review the requested permissions, click Allow, and sign in to your Google account when redirected.

steps.heading: Turn on status syncing
steps.body: Return to Slack, open the Google Calendar app home, and turn on status syncing so your emoji updates automatically during meetings.

faq.question: Will my Slack status change automatically during a meeting?
faq.answer: Yes, once status sync is enabled, Slack updates your emoji and status text based on your calendar events.

faq.question: Can I respond to a meeting invite from Slack?
faq.answer: Yes, rich message cards let you accept, decline, or update your attendance directly in the chat.

faq.question: Does this work with multiple Google Calendars?
faq.answer: You can typically connect one primary account, though some setups support switching between calendars.

faq.question: Can I get a morning summary of my day in Slack?
faq.answer: Yes, the app supports a daily digest notification listing your scheduled meetings.
